assumption_risk_assessment.summary: Key model and resource assumptions introduce risk: DESeq2 pseudobulk inference is association-only (limitations noted), and TF-activity modeling shows multiple QC warnings (genes absent from regulons, non-estimable models, resource discordance, single-resource support). These raise risk that some inferred effects are model/resource artifacts.

confounding_assessment.summary: EXPLORATORY CONFOUNDED-DESIGN ANALYSIS: group and dataset are not independently identifiable. Features are conserved across dataset-level leave-one-out analyses but may still reflect systematic dataset effects.

design_validity_assessment.summary: Design is estimable and full-rank (design formula ~ group, group coefficient 'NBM - NDMM') with substantial replicate counts (NBM:55, NDMM:233) and residual degrees of freedom reported. However, group and dataset are not independently identifiable (exploratory LODO context), which limits validity for causal or dataset-adjusted inference.

generalizability_assessment.summary: The evidence bundle aggregates many represented datasets (see represented_datasets) and large overall cell and replicate counts (deterministic atlas QC), supporting potential generalizability within the included data types. Nevertheless, because dataset and group are not independently identifiable in this exploratory analysis, generalizability beyond the assembled datasets is uncertain without dataset-adjusted or external validation.

limitations: EXPLORATORY CONFOUNDED-DESIGN ANALYSIS: group and dataset are not independently identifiable. Features are conserved across dataset-level leave-one-out analyses but may still reflect systematic dataset effects. (see CAP-DESEQ-003:warnings.EXPLORATORY_CONFOUNDED_DESIGN)
Deterministic evidence does not establish causality; DESeq2 outputs are association-based (see CAP-DESEQ-003:limitations).

TF-activity results include multiple QC warnings (CAP-TF-002:warnings) including genes absent from regulons, non-estimable models, resource discordance, and some signals supported by a single resource; these weaken inference from TF models to robust regulatory conclusions.
reasoning_summary: The bundle contains extensive statistical outputs and evidence of within-bundle replication via LODO conserved features, and the design is estimable with substantial replicate counts. However, the explicit exploratory confounded-design warning (group and dataset not independently identifiable) and multiple TF-model QC warnings substantially reduce confidence in dataset-adjusted or causal claims. The results are useful for hypothesis generation and prioritization but require dataset-adjusted modeling and independent validation before stronger interpretive or translational use.
recommended_follow_up: Run dataset-adjusted differential expression models or a formal meta-analysis that includes dataset as a covariate or uses random-effects to separate group from dataset effects (address the EXPLORATORY_CONFOUNDED_DESIGN warning).
Recompute or validate conserved features in an independent cohort(s) not included in the represented_datasets list (see design_assessment.represented_datasets).

For top candidate genes, inspect per-dataset effect sizes and raw counts (from CAP-DESEQ-003:full_unadjusted_deseq2_results and CAP-DESEQ-003:deseq2_results) to determine whether signals are driven by one or a few datasets.

For TF activity outputs, prioritize TFs with multi-resource consensus and re-run TF analyses after addressing missing regulon coverage (reduce impact of CAP-TF-002:warnings:TF_ACTIVITY_GENES_NOT_IN_REGULONS and TF_ACTIVITY_NON_ESTIMABLE_MODELS).
Consider orthogonal validation (e.g., protein-level assays or targeted experiments) for high-priority findings before downstream mechanistic interpretation.

replication_assessment.summary: Features are conserved across dataset-level leave-one-out (LODO) analyses (evidence of reproducibility across folds) but the overall analysis is exploratory and subject to dataset-level systematic effects. LODO conservation supports within-bundle replication of signals but does not eliminate dataset-driven bias.

statistical_support_assessment.summary: Differential expression was performed with DESeq2 on independent biological pseudobulk replicates, yielding a large number of significant features (reported significant_gene_count). Results and full unadjusted statistics are available for inspection. Statistical testing appears to have been executed and summarized (p-values, fold changes) consistent with the stated pipeline.

strengths: Conserved features across dataset-level LODO analyses (see CAP-DESEQ-003:conserved_features and CAP-DESEQ-003:exploratory_lodo_summary).
Large sample/replicate footprint (deterministic_evidence.atlas_qc reports 167355 cells and eligible_replicates NBM:55, NDMM:233).
Complete DESeq2 outputs and full unadjusted statistics are available for inspection (CAP-DESEQ-003:deseq2_results, CAP-DESEQ-003:full_unadjusted_deseq2_results).

TF-activity modeling was performed with signed regulon resources and includes per-resource TF activity outputs (CAP-TF-002:tf_activity_by_resource, CAP-TF-002:significant_tf_activity), enabling cross-resource QC.
